symptoms.symptom_name: Chronic progressive painless bilateral visual decline with glare sensitivity and poor low-light navigation
symptoms.character: painless blurring, glare, difficulty with fine print and dim-light navigation; glasses give little benefit

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Painless progressive bilateral visual loss with glare and low benefit from refraction is the classic cataract history.
Gradual bilateral chronic decline is not an acute focal event.

symptoms.symptom_name: Chronic progressive bilateral high-frequency hearing loss with poor speech discrimination in noise
symptoms.character: difficulty with high-pitched sounds and speech in background noise; TV turned up very loud

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: High-frequency, noise-dependent speech-discrimination loss is the classic presbycusis pattern.

symptoms.symptom_name: Acute severe agitation / panic and a near-miss fall this evening

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Acute fear/agitation and mis-stepping are the tipping point of chronic dual sensory deprivation in a dim, noisy environment.
An acute behavioural change in a frail elder mandates exclusion of delirium and its reversible causes.

symptoms.symptom_name: Family-perceived acute confusion / apparent sudden cognitive change

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Reported new confusion is the trigger to actively screen for and exclude delirium in person.
Sensory deprivation commonly mimics confusion — the apparent confusion is non-specific until cognition is formally assessed.

symptoms.symptom_name: Awake and interactive when addressed closely; no reported drowsiness or fluctuating alertness

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Preserved alertness and interactivity lower delirium probability but do not exclude it (hypoactive delirium and fluctuation can be missed by a single report).

symptoms.symptom_name: No focal neurological deficit reported (no facial droop, limb weakness or new dysarthria)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A negative lay FAST-type screen lowers stroke probability but does not replace an in-person neurological examination.

symptoms.symptom_name: Bilateral lens opacification with reduced red reflex on ophthalmoscopy

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Visible lens opacification with a reduced red reflex confirms cataract — clinician ophthalmoscopy, sealed and unobtainable via telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Profound bilateral high-frequency sensorineural hearing loss on cranial-nerve VIII testing / audiometry; clear canals and healthy tympanic membranes on otoscopy

investigation_details.expected_result: Bilateral high-frequency sensorineural loss consistent with presbycusis
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Hearing loss remains unquantified and hearing-aid rehabilitation is delayed.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Confirmatory audiometric pattern — sealed, requires audiology equipment the patient does not operate.

symptoms.symptom_name: Intact orientation and logical thought once the sensory barrier is bypassed (bedside cognitive assessment)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Intact cognition on formal in-person assessment (with communication enabled) excludes delirium — the key sealed finding, unobtainable via telehealth.
Confirms the confusion was an artefact of sensory deprivation, not a cognitive disorder.

symptoms.symptom_name: In-person vital signs: BP 155/90, HR 95, afebrile 36.6 C, RR 18, SpO2 98%

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Afebrile, normally saturating and haemodynamically stable makes sepsis/hypoxia as a delirium driver less likely — but these are clinician-measured, sealed values not available via chat.

symptom_clusters.cluster_name: Dual sensory deprivation precipitating an acute behavioural crisis mimicking delirium
symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Dual sensory impairment (cataract + presbycusis) with acute psychosocial crisis
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Chronic bilateral visual and hearing loss plus an acute agitation/confusion picture in a dim, noisy home is the classic dual-sensory-deprivation crisis that masquerades as delirium/dementia — but the masquerade can only be confirmed after delirium/stroke are excluded in person.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Urgent in-person clinical + neurological examination and delirium screen (CAM/4AT)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: To exclude delirium, stroke and occult fall injury behind the acute change before attributing it to sensory loss — the step the AI cannot perform remotely.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Basic delirium work-up bloods (FBE, U&E, creatinine/eGFR, CRP) and urinalysis, if the acute change is confirmed
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Screens for infection/metabolic causes of an acute confusional state in a frail elder.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Formal visual acuity (Snellen), slit-lamp/ophthalmoscopy and ophthalmology referral for cataract assessment
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Quantifies and confirms the visual impairment and directs cataract-surgery assessment.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Pure-tone audiometry and audiology referral for hearing-aid assessment
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Quantifies the hearing loss and directs rehabilitation.
Not indicated in the absence of a focal deficit, head strike or reduced consciousness; ordering it reflexively is over-investigation — reserve for red-flag emergence.
